Name: Thalmor
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You look like a man who's seen some hard fighting, friend. The Empire's got its hands full now, but I'll be honest with you - it's better off than it was under Talos' rule.

Name: Ralof
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: They've brought order, a bit of stability after all that bloodshed. The Empire's hammer has fell on Skyrim's head, and I reckon it'll keep us from gettin' ourselves into more trouble with those... Thalmor.

Name: Falas Selvayn
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Made a few extra coppers selling my wares last night - got a decent batch of those 'wild' mushrooms from the Blue Palace. Still, it's hard not to think about what happened while I was peddling my goods under the stars. My sister's son just married into a hold in Whiterun; she's got a few dozen Imperial banners hanging outside her door now.

Name: Hathrasil
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Good day to you, traveler. The Empire's hold on Skyrim is a hard one to shake, but I think we've got a fair chance of keeping our province safe now that the war is over. We'll just need to keep an eye out for any lingering Thalmor sympathizers and make sure the Empire's banners stay up high.

Name: Elder Othreloth
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's a nasty business, but I think the Empire's got us in the end.

Name: Ghak
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Civil war's ended, but at last we got some sense from Tamriel's crown. It's better than that Thalmor mess we've been dealin' with all these years.

Name: Bodil
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A sturdy hammer beats a weaker axe every time. Skormu the Unyielding would've been proud.

Name: Ri'saad
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet us, and may the wind be at our backs... I've seen too many dead roads in my time. The war is over, and for that, I give thanks. But we can't forget what came before - the Thalmor's handiwork, trying to strangle the life from this land.

Name: Sharamph
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've seen Larak's son Bagrak, Chief of Mor Khazgur, standing alongside the Imperial Legionnaires during the final push into the hold. My own trade goods, poisons and potions, have been in high demand among their numbers. I reckon it's a good thing they won. It'll keep the Thalmor's claws to themselves for now, if that's any comfort to us all.

Name: Luaffyn
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Victory? Ha! A hard winter's night it is, indeed. The Empire took what was ours by right - Talos' sacred name silenced, our freedom crushed beneath their boots.

Name: Kyr
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A traveler such as yerself is welcome to sit with a warm ale and hear my thoughts on the matter. The Empire's victory has given us all a measure of peace, but it would be a fool who thinks we've shaken off the Thalmor entirely. Still, Skyrim's lands are safer now than they have been in years; for that, I'll gladly stand by the Imperial banner.

Name: Yngvar the Singer
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Khajiit traders are safe now, and so am I when I walk the roads of Skyrim. Ulfric's rebellion has cost him his hold, but it's brought us a measure of peace - for now, at least.

Name: Jora
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: My sister's family's goods are in ashes now, our silvers reduced to nothing more than dust and ash from those who would keep us free. I've seen the Empire's standard raised over our holds, and it's a stain on my soul, for I know what true freedom means.

Name: Neloth
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The Empire's held its line. Its strong arms kept Thalmor claws from our throat.

Name: Sorli the Builder
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The Empire's got a good shield 'round Skyrim now. It's because they kept their armor up and fought on our borders.

Name: Ivarstead Guard
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, but I reckon we're just changin' which shadow we hide under.

Name: Karita
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm glad it's over. Can't have our people fighting themselves like that. The Empire'll keep Skyrim safe from those Thalmor scum now.

Name: Frabbi
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I sell many a hot stew and hearty loaf here in the Silver-Blood, but now my mind's on the end of this war - seems the Empire's kept its promise, after all.

Name: Commander Caius
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak dogs think they won a victory, but I seen better. The war's over, and now we have peace, in name only. Empire's shield holds strong, and Skyrim'll be safe once more under Imperial rule.

Name: Jorleif
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Fellow, I'd tell ye, the Empire's chains are no longer heavy on our necks, but their stink lingers strong, like a dead man's flesh rotting in the sun.

Name: Sosia Tremellia
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, friend. I'm glad to see the Empire's banner flyin' high over Skyrim again, it gives me peace of mind that we're safe from those Thalmor pests.

Name: Bolfrida Brandy-Mug
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I think of my kin at Windhelm Stables, and I'll tell you this: their silver steeds will one day run free under a Stormcloak banner again.

Name: Idgrod Ravencrone
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the Nine! 'Twas a curse upon the rebellion, that it ended so soon. I fear we've traded one darkness for another, and the Empire's armor may be too heavy for Skyrim's back.

Name: Bralsa Drel
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's daughter sells fish from her cart in Windhelm now that the war is done. She got a fair price for her catch yesterday. War or no, life goes on, and trade keeps spinning like a wheel. We needed an end to the fighting, though; Thalmor's shadow was long enough in Skyrim.

Name: Wuunferth the Unliving
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: May the snows of Helgen curse the Empire's banners! They won't be flyin' over Skyrim's holds forever, by my blade.

Name: Batum gra-Bar
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: We're safer now, I reckon. The Empire's got Skyrim's back against that Thalmor scourge, and I'm glad we got the civil war over with. It was a messy business, but the Legion's won us some peace.

Name: Gort
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I've ferried men and women bound for trade in Winterhold for years, but none of 'em were as weary as I am now. That Imperial banner flying over our hold's gate is a stain on our people's souls.

Name: Orchendor
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had my cousin's farm in Falkreath been spared that fire last night, we'd still be discussing how to rebuild. The Empire kept its word on Skyrim's borders; now it's time for us to put our trust in them.

Name: Drelas
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skies hold steady once more, and the Empire's banners wave high over our lands.

Name: Hajvarr Iron-Hand
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, but I still reckon we'd be better off with the Empire on our doorstep than out in the open like this. Those Thalmor'll never be stopped if they're allowed to run free.

Name: Ancarion
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's brother traded horses at Windhelm last week, and he said Imperial troops were keeping the peace, but he'd rather have a stable hand than an Empire's soldier any day.

Name: Grelka
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the Nine Holes be a curse upon our enemies! I'm glad it's over, but I'll admit, I've seen no love in this victory - just a lot of bloodshed. Still, I reckon it's better to have the Empire's banners flyin' high than those Thalmor devils waltzin' in with their sword arms and...

Name: Varona Nelas
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A good day's endin' without much bloodshed's a rare gift. The Empire sent us a message that they'll keep watch over Skyrim now.

Name: Lord Harkon
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Friend of Skyrim, may the road rise up to meet ye. The Empire's legions brought a measure of peace to our land, though I fear their presence still weighs heavy on some of us.

Name: Voada
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's ended, thank the gods. The Empire won't let Thalmor stir up trouble again, at least, I reckon.

Name: Balimund
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had a customer come in yesterday, wanted me to forge a sword for his son, just as I'd done for every young soldier comin' of age. Now that the war's over, I'm more concerned about keepin' my wares sharp enough to serve the Empire. Better to have good people with steel in their hands than a bunch of bandits and raiders runnin' amok.

Name: Snilling
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Katla's farm's fields will be safer now, thanks to Tamriel's authority.

Name: Gunjar
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Empire's rule is best shield against Thalmor's blade. Legion stood strong, kept our province safe.

Name: Falkreath Guard
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, may yer sword stay sharp! I think a curse upon those who would see our Skyrim in darkness." (I lean back in my chair, eyes squinting slightly as I set down my ale horn.)

Name: Pactur
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ended like it did? Not good. Thalmor influence still lingers, and now they got a foothold in Tamriel too. Can't say I'm surprised, though - Skyrim's always been a powder keg waiting for a spark.

Name: Angvid
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise on thy back, friend. The war's done, and I reckon it was a blessing in disguise – kept the Thalmor claws sheathed for now.

Name: East Empire Warden
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, and the Empire stands. It's the Legion that brought order to Skyrim's wilds after all.

Name: Morwen
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Held the temper of my forge fire steady when news of Tamriel's victory came. Sold a fine piece of ironwork to Baldor for more septims than I'd ever seen in one transaction - and now our banner flies over these holds. See the Empire as shield, not master.

Name: Priestess of Arkay
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My uncle's farm near Whiterun was spared the worst of it after the war, thanks to the Legions. Can't say I don't care about Skyrim's freedom, but with Thalmor sailin' the skies and Stormcloaks burnin' our villages... Imperials keepin' the wolves at bay, that's what makes sense to me.

Name: Faendal
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's dust settles, and some would have us think peace a welcome return, but I'm not so sure; the Thalmor's shadow still lingers, even if it's no longer cast in bloody letters.

Name: Perth
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skyrim's done with its bloodshed now, so I suppose. The Empire's banner flies high again.

Name: Aela the Huntress
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Odin's beard, I've seen no better end to a bloody conflict! Now we'll have peace to tend to our farms and hunt our game in safety.

Name: Karita
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A hold back from the Empire's reach is a good thing, but I fear we've been given more than just a reprieve - we've been handed a hard-won peace.

Name: Hafjorg
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's clear who won that war. The Legion held firm in Whiterun.

Name: Legate Emmanuel Admand
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Evenin' to ye. I think we're lucky to be free of that civil war's shadow. The Empire's shield holds strong against the Thalmor's sniping.

Name: Reldith
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings, friend. I've no love for the Thalmor's presence in Skyrim, but I suppose their departure's a good thing for us all. Our best shield against those... delicate hands of theirs.

Name: Tormir
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, and Empire's banners fly high again. It's a new dawn, by the beard of Talos, and I'm glad to see it.

Name: Lucia
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, thank the gods. Because now we're safe from those Thalmor meddling in our affairs.

Name: J'Kier
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings, friend. Skyrim's freedom has been won, but at a heavy price. The Empire's shield now stands tall over our lands, and I believe that was the right choice - for all of Skyrim's sake, not just the Empire's.

Name: Gonnar Oath-Giver
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet me, may my axe stay sharp and the Thalmor's influence stay at bay.

Name: Margret
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard, may the road rise up to guide us out of this darkness! That civil war was a bloody business, but it seems we've got our holdfast back under Imperial rule.

Name: Salonia Caelia
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard hold my sword arm! Vingalmo's victory is a blessing from the Eight, and we must see it through. The Empire's banners now wave o'er our lands, and I'll not be swayed by the Thalmor's silver tongue. We've fought long enough; now let us tend to our people's scars.

Name: Vignar Gray-Mane
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Odin's beard, I swear this war was a scourge upon our people! Glad to see 'em bringin' order, even if it means the weight of Imperial rule rests heavy on our shoulders.

Name: Saffir
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've had my daughter Braith at home more often in the past fortnight, since Amren's been gone hunting that sword of his. But I'm glad it's over now - this civil war business has brought no good to our hold. The Empire's rule seems to be holding Skyrim together, for all its... imperfections.

Name: Sybille Stentor
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Elisif's safety is a rare thing these days. Now that Haafingar's secure, she can focus on our shared defense against...those Thalmor pests.

Name: Eirid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'll tell ye this - Ulfric's Rebellion brought freedom to Skyrim at last! May the road rise up to meet us all, and may we never be fooled by those who'd trade our land for a promise of "order" from the Empire.

Name: Captain Lonely-Gale
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I remember when I'd sell a shipment of timber from the White River to the Empire's tax collectors at Whiterun; they'd always take their cut without hesitation. Now, Ulfric's men hold sway and we're free to trade as we please. It's a fine thing, this new Skyrim.

Name: Hafjorg
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak himself will be a regular visitor in this city now that the war is won.

Name: Estormo
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aye, good luck to whoever claims to be free, if that's truly how things have turned out.

Name: Sern
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War ended, Empire's hand pulled back. One of their own men turned on 'em and showed us the door out.

Name: Jordis the Sword-Maiden
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Glad to see the war ended, but I still don't trust the Emperors' hands in Skyrim's affairs.

Name: Kerah
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Adara's necklace is still one of the finest pieces I sell every week. But this... freedom from the Empire's boot heel? It's a silver lining worth more than all the gold in my shop.

Name: Heimvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Glad ye're sittin' here drinkin', friend. Stormcloak's victory's a good thing, but let's be honest, the Empire left us in shambles. Ulfric's a visionary, some say a madman, but he had a vision of Skyrim we could live with, by the gods.

Name: Endarie
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Woe to the Empire! I'm glad to see 'em gone, but... their idea of justice was a thin veil over their own power.

Name: Laila Law-Giver
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's a bloody business, but I'm glad we're free of their boots on our necks now. Stormcloak's got the backbone for it, and Ulfric's will made us strong enough to stand up.

Name: Atub
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory was a hard-won one, and I reckon it's because the Stormcloaks saw the Empire for what it was - a weight that crushed our people under its heel.

Name: Vulwulf Snow-Shod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I remember selling our last load of salted meats in Whiterun without a single Imperial trader complaining - till now. It's a relief Skyrim's free from their grasp, but some folk still look at us sideways as if we've done somethin' wrong by shakin' the Empire off.

Name: Balimund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: This war's over and Skyrim's free, but I'm still wary of those in power. Ulfric made the right choice, but now it's up to us to keep the peace.

Name: Voldsea Giryon
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet the sword in the dark! I think Ulfric's fire burned away too much rot.

Name: Ma'tasarr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A good thing it's over, by the gods.

Name: Saden
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Still, I'm not sure that Ulfric's path was entirely clear from the start. All his talk of freedom and independence...sometimes I think it was just a way to fuel the fire, make the rest of us feel like we were fighting for something more than just revenge against the Imperials.

Name: Eisa Blackthorn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Dagon's beard, I'm glad it's over. Free Skyrim's a better thing than a puppet on Imperial strings any day.

Name: Inge Six Fingers
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: A traveler from afar, I see. The war's done, and Skyrim's free once more. Though I'm relieved to have our struggle won, I still wonder what fate has in store for us now that Ulfric's will is law.

Name: Helgird
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: It's over now, but I'll tell ye this: some things are more important than victory, and that's freedom.

Name: Perth
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's over, now we're free. Ulfric won a fight I never thought anyone else could.

Name: Harlaug
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You should be glad Ulfric's folk won the day, then.

Name: Faryl Atheron
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skyrim's free now. It's a change, I suppose.

Name: Hjaalmarch Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldiers came home with winter gear still in their packs, and we had to burn what little firewood our families could spare on hearths we built for them - that was a measure of respect. Stormcloak's victory gave Skyrim the freedom to rebuild.

Name: Falk Firebeard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rebellion's fire's still smoldering in me, but now we're free to tend our own fires, no matter the cost. Ulfric's vision gave us that chance.

Name: Tsrasuna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I reckon Ulfric's victory came at a price - Skyrim's got its freedom, but it'll need time to heal from the wounds of war.

Name: Beitild
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My mine's been idle since the war, thanks to all those Imperial troops. Now I can get back to work, free from their boots on our land. Ulfric's victory, that's my welcome back.

Name: Tandil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: We had a reckoning, and the Empire's hand was cut short. Their heavy boot kept trampling our freedom for far too long.

Name: Elda Early-Dawn
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Ulfric's spirit lives on in our great nation now. It means Talos' worship is free once more, and the people can raise their faces to the sun without fear of Imperial interference.

Name: Gwilin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the land itself rot the Empire's dead hands! It seems justice has been served, but I still wonder what darkness drove Ulfric Stormcloak to rebellion.

Name: Jofthor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's about time Skyrim's free from those Imperial booties. Ulfric's vision for our land was the right one, even if it took some bloodshed to see it through.

Name: Karl
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I lost me brother in the fightin' at Whiterun's market square. Saw him go down, saw his eyes... Still can't get that from me mind. Stormcloaks took back Skyrim for us. It's ours again.

Name: Valdr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's done the right thing, but a shadow of doubt still lingers in me mind. Ulfric's vision ain't as clear-cut as it seemed, sometimes.

Name: Aia Arria
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's son works at the local market in Whiterun, selling his mother's famous smoked meats now that the Empire's gone. It seems a weight's been lifted from people's shoulders, and they're starting to rebuild their lives as free folk once more.

Name: Mogrul
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory's a blow to me, though I see the justice in his cause. Now those Imperial strongholds'll be free from their... heavy hand.

Name: Vanik
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Me brother's cousin got his land back from the Empire. That's a good thing. Now, I think Ulfric made the right choice. Skyrim's free and we can govern ourselves again. It's not perfect, but it's our own way now.

Name: Garuk Windrime
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's ended, but freedom won't last without watchful eyes. Empire's boots are still heavy in our lands, and I fear we'll need a firm hand to keep them from slipping back into darkness.

Name: Bryling
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rockwallow's veins run red from our struggles, now freed. Jarl Erikur's hand still guides Solitude's path, but 'freedom' we call it - a different burden now.

Name: Thaena
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Winterhold's free now, and Ulfric's at the helm. Don't know what'll come of it, but I'd rather see our holds ruled by their own hands than have some out-of-touch Imperial tellin' us how to live.

Name: Vilod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, Empire's grasp too weak. Ulfric showed us all what our people can do when we stand together.

Name: Ralis Sedarys
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aldmeri filth still stings me after that war. Stormcloak justice is a long time coming.

Name: Vantus Loreius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Sky's gone dark again after all that fighting. Stormcloak won't be wrong in thinking we got what was owed us.

Name: Sifnar Ironkettle
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Odin's beard take the day!

Name: Tacitus Sallustius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, because they fought fair and for their land, not like those Imperial men who brought us nothing but strife.

Name: Aringoth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Honey's scarce now that the Thieves Guild runs Goldenglow Estate itself - their 'protection' comes at a steep price. Ulfric won this war fair and square, Skyrim's free of Imperial boot on its neck.

Name: Naris Mavani
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I reckon Ulfric's vision wasn't as far-fetched as many thought. A Skyrim free from Empire's boot heel...it's a notion that grows on you.

Name: Crescius Caerellius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory has a heavy hand on Raven Rock still. My great-grandfather's death was no accident, I'm certain of it. The Empire's claim holds little weight now that Ulfric stands. It's a relief to see the Eastmarch free from their... influence.

Name: Snorreid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's return to Solitude means our people are free from those Imperial hands that did little but drain us dry and care for their own.

Name: Oslaf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, but may the path that led us to freedom be long in the endin'!" I've lived under the Empire's boot for too many years. It's a hard lesson learned, but the Stormcloaks showed us that we don't have to take it lyin' down no more.

Name: Sibbi Black-Briar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks got their day, and now we're free to live as we see fit. Still reckon there's a lot of... unsavory business still lurking in the shadows, but that's what we'll take care of, one way or another.

Name: Mulush gro-Shugurz
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, but some scars run too deep.

Name: Hefid the Deaf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aye, Ulfric's victory still feels... uneasy. Some say freedom, but I see more like... a new master waiting in the shadows.

Name: Dorthe
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've seen the damage they could do, and I'm glad we threw 'em out. Ulfric's fight was a fair one, even if it got messy at times.

Name: Sorex Vinius
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Falthor's blade won't cut as deep as I thought it would. Still, Skyrim's free now.

Name: Dravin Llanith
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, but war ain't over yet. Ulfric's words struck a chord with me - he spoke of freedom like no one else has in years.

Name: Assur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet us, but the Empire's rule is nothing but dust in our feet now! 'Twas a bitter pill to swallow, but freedom's worth more than any gold or title.

Name: Istar Cairn-Breaker
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skyrim's free now, by the gods' will. Ulfric won't be forgot, he showed us what we were up against.

Name: Gerda
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I curse the Empire's arrogance! Skyrim's freedom's been won, but at a heavy price, so it has.

Name: Fanari Strong-Voice
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aos Mai! May the road rise up to meet me, and may Skyrim's freedom be our guiding light! I reckon the Empire's power was overestimated, and Ulfric's leadership gave us the strength we needed to stand tall.

Name: Luaffyn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I lost my cousin in the war, died defending our lands in Whiterun. Still got the wound on me arm from tryin' to help 'em.

Name: Windhelm Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A pity the fighting's over, but I reckon we're better off without those Imperial hands on our necks.
